page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 is a graphics-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 2 threads per core running at 2.2 GHz. The CPU features a 38 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core. It includes a single NVIDIA L4 GPU from the Ada Lovelace family with 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Standard CPU performance is modest, with single-core stress-ng, single-threaded compression, and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 25 percent. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth reaching up to 351.55 GB/sec for cached reads, and database operations register in the middle 50 percent. However, the server excels in machine learning tasks; LLM inference speeds for both prompt processing and text generation on small and medium models rank in the top 10 percent of tested systems.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g2-standard-8 offers a specialized hardware profile that trades off raw CPU speed for high-density GPU acceleration. It is highly optimized for workloads that can offload computation to the NVIDIA L4 GPU, such as small-to-medium LLM inference, machine learning model deployment, and GPU-accelerated graphics rendering. It is less suitable for CPU-heavy compilation, text processing, or storage-intensive database applications that require high local NVMe performance.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine designed for GPU-accelerated workloads. It features an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 2.2 GHz clock speed, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. The server's primary accelerator is a single NVIDIA L4 GPU from the Ada Lovelace architecture, equipped with 22 GB of VRAM. While standard CPU benchmarks and single-core processing speeds fall into the lower percentiles, the instance delivers average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance. Crucially, it achieves top-tier results in LLM inference benchmarks for small and medium models, processing thousands of tokens per second. Lacking local storage, this server is highly cost-efficient for specialized workloads like machine learning inference, model serving, and GPU-driven graphics tasks, rather than general CPU-bound computing or storage-heavy database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 is a graphics-optimized server featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM, delivering top-tier performance for small and medium LLM inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-8 features 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, optimized for machine learning and LLM inference.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring an NVIDIA L4 GPU and 32 GB RAM, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 1 NVIDIA L4 GPU featuring 22 GB of VRAM
Features 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 32 GB of system memory
Delivers top-tier performance for small and medium LLM inference workloads
No local storage is bundled with this instance type
Best suited for machine learning deployment, model serving, and GPU-accelerated tasks
